ops.title: No-EOF-newline render cases are in-code tests, never on-disk goldens
ops.body: prek's end-of-file-fixer (from the shared cc-skills precommit-base fragment, no testdata exclusion) normalizes every file to end in exactly one newline, so an on-disk golden whose correct content ends WITHOUT a newline cannot survive any commit-hook run. Commit 4229350f's hygiene pass corrupted `note_body_delimiter.md` this way and main CI was red on TestGoldenRender/TestGoldenRoundTrip from bd2d0c51 (2026-07-18T03:31) until the investigation-kind ship removed the golden. The rule: a render case that must end without a trailing newline is pinned as an in-code want-string test (see TestNoteBodyWithoutTrailingNewline); alternatives rejected: excluding testdata/golden in .pre-commit-config.yaml (file is cc-guides-generated; the exclusion belongs upstream in cc-skills fragments and would need the CI-pinned render binary), and newline-terminator tricks in the golden loader (end-of-file-fixer also trims duplicate trailing newlines, so termination schemes stay ambiguous).
